LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302 PLAINTIFFS CHIHEB KOUNI, MOHAMED KOUNI'S MOTION TO "TRANSFER VENUE TO UNLIMITED JURISDICTION" IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. IT IS UNCLEAR FROM COUNSEL'S DECLARATION WHEN HE LEARNED OF THE CASE'S PURPORTED HIGHER VALUE. (SEE CCP 403.040(B)(2).) THE DECLARATION STATES THAT A DOCTOR "RECENTLY EXAMINED PLAINTIFF," AND THE DOCTOR'S REPORT IS SAID TO BE "ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT A," BUT IT IS NOT ATTACHED. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM.
CASE REMOVED FROM PENDING TRIAL DE NOVO CALENDAR SET FOR JURY TRIAL ON JUN-01-2021 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 206 IF OBJECTION: HEARING SET FOR FEB-17-2021 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPT 610

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS CHIHEB KOUNI AND MOHAMED KOUNI'S MOTION TO TRANSFER: PLAINTIFFS' "MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO UNLIMITED JURISDICTION" IS GRANTED AS A MOTION FOR RECLASSIFICATION UNDER CCP 403.040. BASED ON MEDICAL COSTS AND A JUDICIAL ARBITRATION, IT IS CLEAR THAT THE "CASE IS INCORRECTLY CLASSIFIED." (ID.) MOREOVER, THE MOVING PARTIES REPEATEDLY SOUGHT RECLASSIFICATION AS EARLY AS SEPTEMBER 2019, FAILING TO ACHIEVE IT ONLY DUE TO REPEATED FILING ERRORS BY THEIR COUNSEL. (SEE ID.) EVERYONE INVOLVED HAS LONG BEEN AWARE THAT THE CASE WAS INCORRECTLY CLASSIFIED. PLAINTIFFS SHALL PAY THE RECLASSIFICATION FEE. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-20-2021 Vacated

MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar Feb-19-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs CHIHEB KOUNI and MOHAMED KOUNI's MOTION TO TRANSFER:

Plaintiffs' "motion to transfer venue to unlimited jurisdiction" is granted as a motion for reclassification under CCP 403.040. Based on medical costs and a judicial arbitration, it is clear that the "case is incorrectly classified." (Id.) Moreover, the moving parties repeatedly sought reclassification as early as September 2019, failing to achieve it only due to repeated filing errors by their counsel. (See id.) Everyone involved has long been aware that the case was incorrectly classified. Plaintiffs shall pay the reclassification fee.

Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

TDN SWEEP
Off Calendar Jan-29-2021 Set For Trial On Jun-01-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Assigned To Arbitration On Sep-14-20

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue To Unlimited Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Dec-11-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances by ZOOM Video-conferencing:
Attorney, Michael j. Elizondo (Email: melizondo@kernlaw.com) of the law offices of Kern Segal and Murray (Tel: 415.474.1900) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Erica Summan (Email: esumman@burnhambrown.com) of the law offices of Burnham Brown (Tel: 510.835.6836) defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides, on record as reflected in the notes of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFFS CHIHEB KOUNI, MOHAMED KOUNI'S Motion To "Transfer Venue To Unlimited Jurisdiction" is DENIED without prejudice. It is unclear from counsel's declaration when he learned of the case's purported higher value. (See CCP 403.040(b)(2).) The declaration states that a doctor "recently examined Plaintiff," and the doctor's report is said to be "attached as Exhibit A," but it is not attached.

The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com.
